claim: PD-1 triggering on monocytes reduces IL-10 production by monocytes.

result.claim: PD-1 triggering on monocytes reduces IL-10 production by monocytes.
result.reasoning: The claim that "PD-1 triggering on monocytes reduces IL-10 production by monocytes" is refuted by evidence from paper 10648422. This paper explicitly states that "Triggering of PD-1 expressed on monocytes by PD-L1 induced IL-10 production" (Proposition ID: prop_1075). Furthermore, the paper also notes that "PD-1 expression on monocytes correlates with high plasma concentrations of IL-10" (Proposition ID: prop_1074), which further supports the idea that PD-1 triggering leads to increased, not decreased, IL-10 production.

evidence.text: Interleukin-10 (IL-10) is upregulated during HIV infection.
